channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Google says half of all zero-days it tracked in 2025 targeted buggy enterprise tech
item.description: Enterprise software was a major focus of zero-day activity during 2025, with security and networking devices, like firewalls, VPNs, and virtualization platforms, among the top targeted by malicious hackers.

item.title: Netflix to stream its first-ever Formula 1 race weekend in Canada
item.description: Netflix will stream its first-ever F1 race weekend with the Canadian Grand Prix, simulcast alongside Apple TV’s growing Formula 1 broadcast deal.

item.title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.4 with Pro and Thinking versions
item.description: GPT-5.4 is billed as "our most capable and efficient frontier model for professional work."

item.title: ChatGPT just got another brain boost with GPT-5.4 Thinking — and it’s built for bigger, more complex tasks
item.description: GPT-5.4 Thinking adds deeper reasoning and workflow abilities to ChatGPT while GPT-5.3 Instant remains the fast default for everyday use.

item.title: I hope you like spreadsheets, because GPT-5.4 loves them
item.description: OpenAI is releasing a new model today, and like GPT-5.2 before it, GPT-5.4 is all about professional work. OpenAI is calling GPT-5.4 its most capable frontier model for tasks like coding and data analysis. OpenAI claims the new model produced presentations with stronger, more varied aesthetics and made more effective use of its image generation tools.It's also the first model from OpenAI built with native computer-use capabilities, making it better at carrying out tasks across several apps at the same time. When it comes to computer use, one noticeable improvement OpenAI has recorded is the wa...

item.title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.4 with native computer use mode, financial plugins for Microsoft Excel, Google Sheets
item.description: The AI updates aren't slowing down. Literally two days after OpenAI launched a new underlying AI model for ChatGPT called GPT-5.3 Instant, the company has unveiled another, even more massive upgrade: GPT-5.4.Actually, GPT-5.4 comes in two varieties: GPT-5.4 Thinking and GPT-5.4 Pro, the latter designed for the most complex tasks.Both will be available in OpenAI's paid application programming interface (API) and Codex software development application, while GPT-5.4 Thinking will be available to all paid subscribers of ChatGPT (Plus, the $20-per-month plan, and up) and Pro will be reserved for C...

item.title: Canon could launch a game-changing f/1.4 zoom lens this year — but with one likely catch
item.description: A patent application in Japan reveals a range of fast aperture zoom lenses that Canon is exploring, covering full-frame, APS-C, and PowerShot. Here's what we might see

item.title: 'Straight up lies': Anthropic CEO attacks OpenAI's US military announcement in leaked memo — as new report suggests company is back in talks with Pentagon
item.description: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has been having his say about the complicated events of recent days.

item.title: Study on Alt-Med Breast Cancer Treatments Finds a Grim Side Effect: Death
item.description: Over a five-year span, women who used complementary and alternative medicine had a greater mortality rate than those who relied on traditional care.

item.title: Google essuie un revers face au Tribunal de la concurrence
item.description: Google a essuyé un revers dans sa lutte contre l’autorité de concurrence du Canada, qui a allégué que l’emprise du géant technologique sur le monde de la publicité en ligne avait causé un préjudice.

item.title: The National Videogame Museum has acquired the mythical Nintendo Playstation
item.description: It might sound like the stuff of daydreams now, but once upon a time it was briefly the very real intention of Sony and Nintendo to collaborate on a console. Nicknamed the Nintendo PlayStation, the idea was that a new CD-ROM format backed by Sony would be added to the cartridge-based Super NES, resulting in a hybrid console that could play both. The partnership didn’t last long, though, with Nintendo backing out before it ever really got off the ground, announcing that it would instead be working with Philips. Sony decided to make the PlayStation on its own instead, in an act of revenge that y...

item.title: TCL corrige enfin le plus gros défaut de ses écrans « papier »
item.description: TCL a réussi le pari d’associer le confort visuel de sa technologie NXTpaper, qui imite la texture du papier, aux performances colorimétriques de l’Amoled.

item.title: ‘You can see someone going to the toilet, or getting undressed’ — contractors warn your Meta AI glasses might see more than you realize
item.description: Meta contractors are seeing a lot more from our smart glasses than we might realize.

item.title: The Boys season 5 final trailer reveals Homelander's unsettling plan for world domination — but it evades my biggest question about the Prime Video show's last chapter
item.description: Prime Video has released the official trailer for The Boys' fifth and final season ahead of its early April debut.

item.title: Epic Games CEO Tim Sweeney Agrees to Put on a Muzzle Until at Least 2032
item.description: Epic and Google have called a truce in their years-long legal battle.

item.title: « Du haut débit partout dans le monde » : comment le Rivian R2 vient de réinventer l’eSIM automobile
item.description: Rivian annonce plusieurs nouveautés autour de son SUV électrique R2, et notamment une eSIM pensée pour l'automobile permettant de se connecter partout dans le monde et ainsi ne plus être tributaire des zones blanches où les fonctionnalités des voitures connectées pourraient tout simplement ne plus fonctionner.

item.title: Best Buy is already discounting Apple's brand-new devices — score discounts on the MacBook Neo, iPad Air, and MacBook Pro
item.description: The MacBook Neo, iPad Air M4, and MacBook Pro M4 are available to preorder, and Best Buy is offering incredible discounts, up to $250 in savings.

item.title: Kensington SD7100T5 EQ Pro docking station review: Easier to buy than a laptop or PC that has the ports you need for the full Thunderbolt 5 experience
item.description: The Kensington SD7100T5 EQ Pro Thunderbolt 5 Dock doubles down on features, but you pay more for it.

item.title: AdGuard enters the metaverse: VPN and ad-blocker extensions launch on Meta Quest
item.description: Browsing in VR just got cleaner and safer. AdGuard has launched two new extensions for the Meta Quest browser, bringing ad-blocking and VPN privacy to your headset. Here’s how to install them.

item.title: La très bonne enceinte Middleton notée 9/10 par nos soins s’accompagne du casque Major V dans un pack à moins 30 %
item.description: Avec la Marshall Middleton, vous aurez à disposition une superbe enceinte endurante. Pour voyager, vous pourrez compter sur le confortable Major V dans un pack vendu à 184,99 euros chez Cdiscount.

item.title: Google begins calling out battery-killing Android apps
item.description: Google is living up to its word and posting warning labels for battery-killing apps. 9to5Google spotted Google's rollout announcement, which the company previously said would arrive on March 1.The label says, “This app may use more battery than expected due to high background activity.” If you don't yet see the warnings, they may not have reached you yet. Google says the banners will "roll out gradually to impacted apps" in the coming weeks.Play Store battery warningGoogleWarning labels aren’t the only stick in Google’s fight against infringing apps. They may also be excluded from discovery se...

item.title: ‘The American people should not be footing the bill for the benefit of private companies': Tech giants sign White House pledge to not pass on data center electricity costs to consumers - here's everything you need to know
item.description: The Ratepayer Protection Pledge has been signed by numerous hyperscalers and AI companies, and seeks to prevents American consumers footing the bill for increased energy demand due to data centers.

item.title: Apple Blocks US Users From Downloading ByteDance’s Chinese Apps
item.description: In January, after TikTok announced a deal to transfer its US operations, Apple began blocking people in the US from downloading or updating ByteDance apps designed for the Chinese market.

item.title: 'A liability trap' — NordVPN slams Utah age verification law targeting VPN users
item.description: Utah lawmakers are a step closer to enforcing a new age verification law that NordVPN warns could "punish all VPN users globally." Here's everything you need to know about the controversial bill.

item.title: The First Trailer for the Final Season of ‘The Boys’ Heralds the End of a Bloody Era
item.description: A diabolical farewell approaches when the series finale of 'The Boys' lands on Prime Video this April.

item.title: 1 036 km d’autonomie, recharge en 6 minutes et prix plancher : le break électrique Denza Z9 GT qui va secouer l’Europe
item.description: La filiale premium de BYD, Denza, vient de lancer une version restylée de son break sportif électrique, avec des chiffres qui donnent encore plus le vertige par rapport à ceux de la précédente version, le tout avec une promesse : la voiture électrique avec la plus grande autonomie au monde. Mais avec une petite subtilité cependant.

item.title: The Bargain-Basement MacBook Neo Exposes the Insane Price of Another Key Apple Product
item.description: Can it really be the case that the company can now make a laptop $200 cheaper than a watch?

item.title: Cursor is rolling out a new kind of agentic coding tool
item.description: Called Automations, the new system gives users a way to automatically launch agents within their coding environment, triggered by a new addition to the codebase, a Slack message, or a simple timer.

item.title: OpenAI’s Pentagon deal once again calls Sam Altman’s credibility into question
item.description: Altman was supportive of Anthropic in its contract dispute with the Pentagon, but all the while he was positioning his company to supplant its rival’s AI models with its own. Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week via email here....

item.title: J’ai testé les Qwen Glasses : bien plus qu’une copie des Meta Ray-Ban
item.description: On a enfin un vrai concurrent aux Meta Ray-Ban. Alibaba débarque avec ses Qwen Glasses et, sur le papier, la proposition est solide.

item.title: The rare Nintendo PlayStation hardware prototype that was never released has been acquired by The National Videogame Museum
item.description: The National Videogame Museum has acquired the oldest Nintendo PlayStation hardware prototype that exists.

item.title: Roblox is censoring chats with AI
item.description: Roblox is using AI to alter the content of chat messages on its platform in real time using a new feature rolling out today. Real-time chat rephrasing goes beyond the current filtering for banned language, which replaces certain words and phrases with "#" symbols. Now, Roblox says those words and phrases can be "translated into […]

item.title: Adobe Lightroom Classic (2026) review: Perfect photo organization with time-saving AI at it's best - but it can be hit and miss
item.description: Adobe’s Lightroom Classic is a powerful tool to catalog, review and edit your shots, and the latest version adds more AI tools to it, from the highly useful to the somewhat questionable. Let’s check out what’s new.

item.title: Switch 2 : les joueurs achètent moins de jeux et c’est un gros problème pour Nintendo
item.description: Malgré le succès de la Switch 2, l'horizon de Nintendo se remplit de petits nuages noirs qui pourraient se transformer en grosse tempête. La flambée des prix de la mémoire qui touche toute l'industrie a des répercussions inattendues pour le constructeur japonais.

item.title: Meta sued over AI smart glasses’ privacy concerns, after workers reviewed nudity, sex, and other footage
item.description: Lawyers say Meta's marketing materials promised privacy and user control over sharing footage. But an investigation found that subcontractors are reviewing footage from customers' glasses.

item.title: Google pledges roughly three hours of its annual profit to fight climate change
item.description: Google has pledged "at least" $50 million through 2030 on "projects designed to eliminate superpollutants." The company will be joined by a handful of other companies including Amazon and Salesforce in the newly formed Superpollutant Action Initiative. In total, these companies have committed $100 million to the project, hoping to "accelerate the reduction" of superpollutants like methane, black carbon and refrigerant gases. Google says these are responsible for close to half of all planetary warming. "Superpollutants are a major part of the equation to limit atmospheric warming. Experts agree...

item.title: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei could still be trying to make a deal with Pentagon
item.description: Anthropic's $200 million contract with the Department of Defense broke down due to disagreements over giving the military unrestricted access to its AI.

item.title: Daylight saving time 2026: A major part of Canada just made DST permanent. Why these U.S. states could follow
item.description: It’s almost time for Americans to ‘spring forward’ this Sunday, reigniting debate over whether we should keep changing the clocks. Daylight savings time (DST) is just around the corner. This Sunday, March 8, the clocks will spring forward again, and with the change comes the ongoing conversation about, well—why are we doing this, anyway? According to an AP-NORC poll, only 12% of Americans favor DST, while 47% oppose it and 40% are neutral....

item.title: Canadian government says OpenAI will take further steps to strengthen safety protocols
item.description: The Canadian government says that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has agreed to take steps to immediately strengthen safety protocols, according to a report by Wall Street Journal. This follows a mass shooting incident at a high school in which OpenAI flagged the suspect and suspended his account, but did not alert authorities. These changes look to primarily involve law enforcement, with commitments to notify police about potentially suspicious use of ChatGPT. We don't have any confirmation from the company at this time, but Canada's Artificial Intelligence Minister Evan Solomon says he "asked OpenAI t...

item.title: Oura acquires Doublepoint, a startup that specializes in gesture recognition technology
item.description: The company believes its next phase of wearable AI will be powered by a combination of voice and gesture.

item.title: Boeing’s Contribution to NASA’s Moon Program Just Took a Major Hit
item.description: A pending contractor switchup marks another blow to Boeing, which is struggling to prove itself as a reliable strategic partner to NASA.

item.title: Best Buy has launched a huge Apple Shopping Event: Shop new MacBooks, iPads, and more all weekend
item.description: Best Buy is running an Apple Shopping Event. Shop now and score savings on the brand new MacBook Neo and iPad Air with M4 chip.

item.title: Oura buys gesture-navigation startup DoublePoint
item.description: Smart ring maker Oura has acquired Doublepoint, a company that specializes in gesture recognition for wearables. The Finnish startup uses smartwatches and wristbands as examples of products that benefit from its technology, but Oura will clearly be looking to incorporate it into its rings, in theory allowing you to control your connected devices with hand movements. Oura said in a press release that the deal sees it inherit an "exceptional team of AI architects and builders from Doublepoint," including Doublepoint's four founders. The newly-acquired company will remain in its native Helsinki, ...

item.title: Meta’s AI glasses reportedly send sensitive footage to human reviewers in Kenya
item.description: Meta's AI-powered smart glasses could be sending sensitive footage to human reviewers in Nairobi, Kenya, according to an investigation by the Swedish outlets Svenska Dagbladet and Göteborgs-Posten. The report, which was published last week, claims Meta contractors in Kenya have seen videos captured with the smart glasses that show "bathroom visits, sex and other intimate […]...

item.title: A defiant Elon Musk takes the stand in a jury trial over Twitter takeover
item.description: It’s not the first time Musk has had to defend himself in court against allegations of deceptive practices. A defiant Elon Musk on Wednesday took the stand in a jury trial to defend himself against accusations that he engaged in a pattern of deceptive behavior that misled investors as he attempted to back out of his $44 billion deal to buy Twitter before he finally completed the takeover.The civil trial in San Francisco centers on a class-action lawsuit filed just before Musk took control of Twitter, a social media service he renamed X, in October 2022, six months after agreeing to buy the emb...

item.title: Chinese hackers hide malware within Windows and Google Drive to hit government targets
item.description: An APT41 spinoff is spying on targets across Europe and Asia using a blend of custom and legitimate tools.

item.title: If you're buying a MacBook Neo, this $2.49 monthly add-on is a smart way to stay secure
item.description: A new MacBook is amazing, so make sure to protect your data

item.title: The new LG C6 and G6 OLED TVs get prices, and it's good news — even the new brighter C6 models aren't more expensive
item.description: LG's pricing for the new LG C6 and G6 OLED TVs brings good news: the prices are the same or better than last year

item.title: Nothings new over-ear headphones arrive with an almost unbelievable battery life and mid-range price
item.description: Nothing officially announced the $199 Headphone (a) at Mobile World Congress in Barcelona. The new over-ears promise 135 hours of battery.

item.title: Sonos’ Comeback Could Start With Two New Bluetooth Speakers
item.description: Don't call it a comeback. Or do, actually?

item.title: Osmo is trying to crack AR edutainment (again)
item.description: This is Lowpass by Janko Roettgers, a newsletter on the ever-evolving intersection of tech and entertainment, syndicated just for The Verge subscribers once a week. I still remember the first time I tried the kids edutainment system Osmo back in 2014: I was sitting in front of an iPad, placed vertically on a white iPad […]

item.title: Best phones of MWC 2026: I just tested 30 phones in 2 days and these are the 6 you absolutely must see, including one I have to have
item.description: Best phones of MWC 2026: 6 handsets you need to own.

item.title: Munbyn RealWriter 405B dual-color thermal printer review: More expensive than I'd like, but performance and build quality are excellent
item.description: Munbyn’s thermal printer now prints in color - kind of.

item.title: Netflix buys Ben Affleck’s AI filmmaking company InterPositive
item.description: InterPositive isn't trying to make AI actors or synthetic performances. Rather, the company has created a model that helps production teams work with footage from their own productions to help make edits in post-production.

item.title: L’Asus Rog Xbox Ally est de retour en forte promotion : plus de 100 € de réduction pour cette console portable qui renferme un vrai PC
item.description: Bonne nouvelle pour ceux qui espéraient une baisse de prix sur la dernière console d’Asus en partenariat avec Microsoft, la ROG Xbox Ally : elle est désormais proposée à 491 euros au lieu de 599 euros.

item.title: 10 Years Later, ‘Channel Zero: Candle Cove’ Is as Haunting as Ever
item.description: Shudder has all four seasons of the Syfy series, which means a reunion with Jawbone is just a stream away.
